events.text: Patient is a 71-year-old male of Hispanic/Latino ethnicity (White race), BMI 24.1, with a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and former tobacco use (30 pack-years, quit 10 years ago).
At age 70, patient presented with left lower quadrant pain and partial bowel obstruction; CT revealed a descending colon mass (C18.6) with hepatic metastases.
Colonoscopic biopsy of the descending colon mass showed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with focal signet ring cell features.

Comprehensive genomic profiling identified KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF wild-type, no PIK3CA mutation, TP53 R175H mutation, APC truncating mutation; tumor was microsatellite stable (MSS) with intact mismatch repair proteins.

CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 70 showed descending colon mass causing partial obstruction, four hepatic metastases involving both lobes (largest 5.6 cm in segment VIII), and scattered subcentimeter pulmonary nodules too small to characterize; staged T4bN1aM1a (liver).
At age 70, baseline CEA was 224 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 196 U/mL; given bulky bilobar liver disease and left-sided primary with KRAS mutation, palliative systemic therapy recommended.
At age 70, patient started first-line FOLFIRI (irinotecan 180 mg/m2, 5-FU 400 mg/m2 bolus and 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours, leucovorin 400 mg/m2) plus bevacizumab 5 mg/kg every 2 weeks.
After cycle 2 of FOLFIRI-bevacizumab, patient developed grade 3 diarrhea requiring hospitalization for IV hydration and electrolyte repletion; irinotecan reduced to 150 mg/m2.
At age 70, patient experienced grade 2 hypertension (BP 162/98) attributed to bevacizumab, managed with lisinopril 20 mg daily.

Restaging CT after 6 cycles of FOLFIRI-bevacizumab showed mixed response: two hepatic lesions decreased in size but two new subcentimeter lesions appeared in segment IV; pulmonary nodules stable; CEA decreased to 98 ng/mL.
At age 71, tumor board assessed overall stable to mixed response; decision to continue FOLFIRI-bevacizumab with close monitoring given lack of better alternatives for KRAS-mutant disease.
At age 71, patient developed grade 2 alopecia on FOLFIRI which was distressing but did not require dose modification.
CT at age 71 after 12 cycles showed progressive disease with enlarging hepatic metastases (largest now 6.8 cm) and new peritoneal implants; CEA rose to 312 ng/mL.
At age 71, CA 19-9 increased to 287 U/mL; ECOG performance status declined to 2 due to fatigue and abdominal discomfort.
At age 71, patient transitioned to second-line regorafenib 160 mg daily on a 3-weeks-on/1-week-off schedule.
At age 71, patient developed grade 3 hand-foot skin reaction on regorafenib, requiring dose reduction to 120 mg daily and supportive care with urea cream.
At age 71, patient experienced grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 elevation of liver transaminases (AST 72, ALT 68) on regorafenib.
CT after 2 cycles of regorafenib showed continued progression of hepatic and peritoneal disease; CEA 445 ng/mL; discussed transition to best supportive care or TAS-102.
At age 71, patient started TAS-102 (trifluridine/tipiracil) 35 mg/m2 twice daily on days 1-5 and 8-12 of each 28-day cycle as third-line therapy.
At age 71, patient referred to palliative care for symptom management alongside TAS-102 therapy; goals of care discussion documented preference for continued treatment.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
Colonoscopic biopsy of the descending colon mass showed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with focal signet ring cell features.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
Comprehensive genomic profiling identified KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF wild-type, no PIK3CA mutation, TP53 R175H mutation, APC truncating mutation; tumor was microsatellite stable (MSS) with intact mismatch repair proteins.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 70 showed descending colon mass causing partial obstruction, four hepatic metastases involving both lobes (largest 5.6 cm in segment VIII), and scattered subcentimeter pulmonary nodules too small to characterize; staged T4bN1aM1a (liver).

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 70, baseline CEA was 224 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 196 U/mL; given bulky bilobar liver disease and left-sided primary with KRAS mutation, palliative systemic therapy recommended.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
Restaging CT after 6 cycles of FOLFIRI-bevacizumab showed mixed response: two hepatic lesions decreased in size but two new subcentimeter lesions appeared in segment IV; pulmonary nodules stable; CEA decreased to 98 ng/mL.

Please correlate clinically.

At age 71, tumor board assessed overall stable to mixed response; decision to continue FOLFIRI-bevacizumab with close monitoring given lack of better alternatives for KRAS-mutant disease.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
CT at age 71 after 12 cycles showed progressive disease with enlarging hepatic metastases (largest now 6.8 cm) and new peritoneal implants; CEA rose to 312 ng/mL.

At age 71, CA 19-9 increased to 287 U/mL; ECOG performance status declined to 2 due to fatigue and abdominal discomfort.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CT after 2 cycles of regorafenib showed continued progression of hepatic and peritoneal disease; CEA 445 ng/mL; discussed transition to best supportive care or TAS-102.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 71, patient referred to palliative care for symptom management alongside TAS-102 therapy; goals of care discussion documented preference for continued treatment.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation. Left-sided and right-sided primary tumors. Treated with FOLFOX or FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab. Some with liver-limited metastases considered for hepatic resection. MSI/MMR testing performed.
